title: asplenium lunulatum subsp. pteropus
intro: Asplenium lunulatum subsp. pteropus, also known as the fan-leaved spleenwort or the fan spleenwort, is a subspecies of fern that belongs to the family Aspleniaceae. It is native to Australia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating various skin conditions, including wounds and burns. In herbal preparations, Asplenium lunulatum subsp. pteropus is often used as a tincture or infused in hot water to make a tea, and its leaves can also be dried and used in herbal remedies.
